title: cyperus malaccensis subsp. monophyllus
intro: Cyperus malaccensis subsp. monophyllus, also known as the single-leaf umbrella sedge, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cyperus malaccensis subsp. monophyllus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
